topic: MCP governance layers in Grace-Mar (policy registry, authority bindings, receipts)
operator_intent: Summarize how MCP capability classes connect to authority surfaces so future tooling stays auditable without implying Record merge.

sources.title: Governed MCP layer overview
claims: MCP integration is planning/policy only by default.
Merge authority stays with the companion and governed pipeline.
short_excerpts: Grace-Mar separates interface/runtime assistance from canonical durable state.
MCP outputs belong on the runtime / WORK side until promoted through the gate.

sources.title: Authority bindings documentation
claims: Each output_lane maps to one authority_surface from authority-map.json.
short_excerpts: Bindings pin lanes to authority surfaces so capability posture cannot widen silently.
sources.notes: Cross-check when proposing new MCP-shaped tooling.

candidate_claims.claim: Structured receipts should precede any future live MCP wiring for auditability.
Lane-to-surface bindings reduce drift between assistant UX labels and authority classes.

risks_or_uncertainties: Operator workflow adoption depends on discipline; stubs remain non-canonical until promoted.
Future capability IDs must stay aligned with bindings YAML when registry edits land.
